title: 'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
subhead: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Gilles Paris.

title: War in Iran: 'Sailors are not just stuck in the Strait of Hormuz, they are prisoners on their ships'
subhead: Nearly 20,000 sailors remain stranded in the Arabian-Persian Gulf as Tehran blocks the maritime corridor. With international tensions escalating, seafaring is becoming far more perilous, writes Julien Bouissou.

title: Fifteen years after Fukushima and 40 years after Chernobyl, what have we learned?
subhead: Between changing perceptions of risk or developing new equipment to cope with extreme hazards, major disasters have had a profound impact on nuclear safety management.

title: PSG Hit Chelsea for Five in Champions League Last-16 First Leg
subhead: Having struggled in recent matches, PSG secured a brilliant 5–2 victory on Wednesday in the first leg of the Round of 16. The Parisians were able to count on a decisive contribution from substitute Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, establishing a substantial advantage heading into the return leg.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhead: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

title: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
subhead: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.

title: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhead: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

title: Anoush Ganjipour, philosopher: 'The Iranian people remain the first and last losers of the war in the Middle East'
Trump's war gets caught in the oil trap
Dominique Eddé, Lebanese writer: 'The present moment is overwhelming; it is one of destruction. All that remains is to think and to stand tall'
